textByHash.1c6f8136a70822356b00c45b50620e9f6bf96251: It's been so long since I've heard another person's voice.
textByHash.9290e0f01c20f2c5a4cfb381e86174e60abd195c: That was one of the guardians of this once great city.
textByHash.6fd4486a3f69c1e43322b84daeb1e39be65c8b8a: Yes! Camdozaal, home of the Imcando Dwarves.
textByHash.8e95288c799fee1e58e39965d21af3b885ca598e: These halls once bustled with life, but that was a long time ago. I'm the only one left now. The name's Ramarno.
textByHash.92a8745aaf75d55bf64bb869b86ee006121457c3: Not much of a city anymore, I suppose.
textByHash.64a47662ebe9c99a5635790569e3864fa7363001: We have plenty more. No real harm done. That said, I do think I might have a way for you to make up for it. There'll likely be something in it for you as well.
textByHash.2eee4bc054be7fdef3a6d7f468a096e8685f4e9f: My workshop is just north of here. Meet me there and we'll talk further.
textByHash.5400046ab1033c036dd50f9f2a196c9dff968d31: Ah. I'm sure you have plenty of questions. I will do my best to answer them.
textByHash.dec15a5df4c05ea863f46d7556fda456832760ea: A shield? What kind of shield?
textByHash.63ff4f451d7cd9015bed88e3cb10b0fc7d8ea497: Ah, and you are hoping to use it to do so again. I wish I could help you, but I know little of the shield and its power. It was created far before my time.
textByHash.3fd6671a2807b87705c21cac1ce96e06afa945bf: Still, I may have a way for you to learn what you need.
textByHash.21b3c12f902e7446f734f6a50825b649d791714c: The Sacred Forge is far more than a mere furnace! It is a powerful relic from a time when the gods still walked these lands.
textByHash.9a2b0ef6f165bd6e73c8b0eb9cbdbac51223e326: Before Camdozaal's ruin, our sages would use the forge to guide our people. It is said the forge granted them access to the Dream Theatre, a place where they were blessed with visions of the past, present and future.
textByHash.34adcb08c6c012714210d880865e7aac8f533f3d: The Forge will need powering, but normal barronite won't work for this. You'll need to obtain some imbued barronite. With that, provided the forge deems you worthy, you will be able to enter the Dream Theatre.
textByHash.641d45c5ff0604b7ef1205144ca436cca8950ac0: Head to the mines and mine yourself a barronite deposit. Once you have that, imbue it using the core of a chaos golem from the golem workshop.
textByHash.6e5a85c87b7b62684c7b0ee59cc779cbff749911: You'll need to obtain some imbued barronite. Head to the mines and mine yourself a barronite deposit. Once you have that, imbue it using the core of a chaos golem from the golem workshop.
textByHash.33a99fbc68de4d2b9da3962bb3963184484ddf71: I see you've got the imbued barronite. Just place it into the Sacred Forge. Provided the forge deems you worthy, you'll then be able to enter the Dream Theatre.
textByHash.21d70feaf8211fedb8e2dc730aff434bf6be14c8: Human archeologists? Yes, some showed up recently. I told them they'd be fine to look around as long as they were careful. They're probably over by the bank chest.
textByHash.591fd1959181b979ed8ed072967ad0e497f1900f: Ah . I'm sure you have plenty of questions. I will do my best to answer them.
textByHash.2e4b9b154c9c9e18954429e9fb82028bc56e986a: I did, long ago. I thought she was gone. Tell me... was she alone?
textByHash.761a13aa8873af911c24b5132f63d490cdfa2851: That's good. I hope I get to meet her soon.
textByHash.70d7e4057e71e2c67851a34e5d4980e51ed1dde0: Ah... It's a tough tale to tell. Even though it's been many years since the last of my kin died out, the memory is still fresh.
textByHash.8d4d6e66d898075f2debbaf2b848669ed03bac2e: No, I will. The legacy of my kin must live on.
textByHash.3975799667a2ef88e93cf4e7d9736e7779b473e0: As you can see around you, Camdozaal was once a great city. Alive with the hum of activity. We dug deep into the mountain and reaped the rewards.
textByHash.a5472c82ff73f15ac90e0df3ec2fc302642c800e: Our methods would often differ from other dwarves. Whereas they just used traditional smithing techniques, we would combine them with magic as well.
textByHash.dc4a17ed83a89dc079584153d06b8aeb58a3182d: Alas, that was ultimately our undoing. We learned of a horde of barbarians pillaging across the land, hunting down and killing anyone who dared use magic.
textByHash.63eb8dccd949fcf6dad9b7b4821234f3bace2142: We underestimated them. We believed our weapons would be enough to keep us safe. We were wrong.
textByHash.1c1e419951ff94d2279cdf13249650bda6b4919e: The barbarians brought ruin upon Camdozaal. Most of my kind died as the city collapsed around them. Only a few of us survived until the end. Now, only I remain.
textByHash.c05ed445f7b59f08332a27a43ebb4f3477074f46: I'm glad they didn't win, I suppose. But it doesn't change much for me. The Imcando are all but extinct. I am one of the last to remain.
textByHash.2df9de1dd65203266c7f6a9aaad323000e51df52: Yes... You're right. I should not be so down. There is still a chance my kind can live on.
textByHash.b8d3d8f4ecd64bfe893177687cf6b4c2c9360893: I did, long ago. Is she...
textByHash.38f6366c43ae4466ab10f1442b08e2ad50501e22: I'm sorry, , but let us not speak of this further. To learn of the death of another of my kin is no surprise, but it pains me all the same. I would prefer to focus my thoughts elsewhere.
textByHash.48aee04ac0eff32ecf93c2ac07d8e2d8d1020bbf: What? But then... what happened to her?
textByHash.6a6d6e09ad53cb2dd280cf43e82c04fabe72c5a4: Camdozaal is a shadow of what it once was. We Imcando Dwarves were the finest artisans in the world, but now I am all that remains. Today, you'll only find echoes of our once great city.
textByHash.685a002ad7604a1289f65ec6185c2e69def7c733: But that which was once great can still be great again! We Imcando Dwarves know that better than most. There is hope for the future of Camdozaal. But I need help.
textByHash.cdec6feacd5fd455841b05bef84a5c5d7f4526bf: We have a Sacred Forge. You will find none other like it in all the world. Some of the greatest weapons of legend started life here.
textByHash.895d4c37060a4fbfc561b34dd3a950a715b26487: The Forge could be the key to Camdozaal's future, but it's going out. If it does, I fear it will never be lit again. I can no longer gather the fuel it needs. But perhaps you can?
textByHash.df79a43a4f6b499f250d4bbb36848b2eb58bb83a: Barronite. You'll only find it in these ruins. If you can gather some for me, I can use it to keep the Forge lit. In return, I'll let you take what you want from our Great Vault.
textByHash.84d1ed179ad35d16f376d9def887f7d8f5eb2755: I no longer have use for anything in the Vault. Besides, material possessions mean nothing compared to the fire of the Sacred Forge.
textByHash.945fdac29d884deec7a3774fb4afd4f762f92324: Anywhere you like. The ruins are filled with barronite, so anything you do here should yield some. If you need more information on specific things you can do, just let me know.
textByHash.ccf7dfb19ae68f73cf353ccedfef2c082f25c688: I'm glad they didn't win, I suppose. But it doesn't change much for me. I am still the last Imcando Dwarf.
textByHash.e40ebbe9b9139926855a1793dce14f0ff45a1d0b: Thank you. That gives me some hope. If you have any more questions, do not hesitate to ask.
textByHash.ed34082a096abadf8f00e1608db0714c7cd08e4e: As you can see, Camdozaal isn't the city it once was. That said, there are still a few things around here that might interest you. There's the golem workshop, the barronite mine and the offering altar.
textByHash.09bd8ae02912e0d125884a963cb8ab344cccc6bd: You'll find it to the east. It was once the forefront of our technological advances. Sadly, it was also one of the major factors that led to our doom.
textByHash.fed1450344593214664b2cdf7c7c6efe3f7e2e7a: Camdozaal was built upon the ruins of an older civilization. Little trace of them remained by the time we arrived. However, when building the city, ancient golems were discovered.
textByHash.be3947b6d7312224f7fc61cd030525a0fcac5468: These golems were very crude. They were mostly made from clay, of all things. We used our advanced Smithing knowledge to improve their form. Eventually, we were able to get them working again.
textByHash.3b89f428106ba756828d7d3b4d74fc1714772eb9: We kept pushing with the golems. Many of them we imbued with magical energies. We started with mind and body energy, but eventually moved on to chaos as well.
textByHash.151e4ffdffe63323db91771d6ef4efeddd1781de: Alas, the influence of chaos had grave results. We lost control of many. Those that became corrupt had to be locked away, but by then it was already too late.
textByHash.ac10ed3316f891cdc07a4287a3176c25e758ef85: The barbarians had learnt of our experimentation, and were determined to destroy all traces of it.
textByHash.7ab68a21d1f804a7ef583bb572c8ee0dee1873be: Ironically, locking them away meant the golems were among the few things to survive the barbarians. If you fancy a bit of combat, you might want to go take a look.
textByHash.bb51be554267f61f98a54454c3ec67824033cf33: They're largely made of barronite now, so they're a good source of the stuff.
textByHash.216d0b8f211b31a3dd3a50018660c2b9442a02e3: Their cores are also very interesting. Apparently, the dwarves of the golem workshop once used old cores to craft runes. Maybe you could do the same.
textByHash.dc5d777ee90616a8804e0ad5b9fb98468dbc643c: You'll find it to the north west. Unsurprisingly, it's the best place to find barronite in the city.
textByHash.cc6f21547dc2bd63da4a506a8774eccb98a2ce8f: At one time, our miners would work all through the day there to gather the barronite needed to power the Sacred Forge. That was a long time ago.
textByHash.f3fca987dff6a4d79c8d9382059b734b6a1f7a7c: If you're lucky, you might find other things while mining there. We were not the first to build a home beneath this mountain. Sometimes traces of an older civilization can be found.
textByHash.e711493d1a4bdecdd82544802a7b8911483ca863: You might find some ancient relics encased within barronite. If you do, you can use the anvils here to crack open the deposits.
textByHash.6d0ac60ac3dac99c886651c5aaaa468ea360b995: The relics are of no interest to me. Maybe some of your race would find them interesting.
textByHash.c017a7510d2f4a397f88c5cd758e9036ce955e59: You'll find it to the south west. It's not something that ever interested me, but most of my kind were followers of Saradomin - the god of order and wisdom.
textByHash.563af0cd546c99a119afd220664f4a58fa2ffad0: Many of my kin would visit the altar to make offerings in his name.
textByHash.bb6bc5f4392fd78e07eaa5a2cf677db04e564dbb: First, they'd catch fish from the nearby reservoir. Next, they'd ritually prepare them at tables by the altar. Finally, they'd use the altar to offer the fish.
textByHash.f8dc4ca6de478a6fc5deccf1e7beea6b6aaf2a62: There's plenty of barronite to be found within the reservoir, so it might be worth checking out. Be warned though: worshipping gods never did my kind any good.
textByHash.46ef860f2f33be4ef98095b7d3e879be61e1ca36: Well hand it over and I'll take a look.
textByHash.fa2e224d328fe6b5f89e2ffab01117da45ffb179: An Imcando Dwarf survived outside the city?
textByHash.68c30856c8a252f672fb6efe05449edaf218a1f5: I can't believe it. I hope to meet him one day. He must be a truly amazing smith to reach such heights.
textByHash.a088bd6ef55a936ccf356569584e9f04fc17d832: Thank you! You've given me hope that my kin can live on. If you need help with anything else, let me know.
textByHash.09b3e2ef8066a32a0eff8deff785fac9089b36c6: I'm afraid not. The Sacred Forge is like none other in existence. Only an Imcando Dwarf could use it correctly.
textByHash.cc3eba7c7879e9dd4da80af049ce1bc902b32f41: Truth be told, I don't fully know. It's far older than I am. It was supposedly built in the earliest days of the city.
textByHash.77cb9ebb797cb0da592ec71b63f8ce2062f304af: What I do know is that it's been imbued with powerful magic - some said that of the gods themselves. The Forge powers the entire city, or what's left of it at least.
textByHash.54a360bee76977a3b5ef47809032cd33c1a25ca0: I've been doing my best to keep it lit over the years. It's not a job I'm capable of any more though. That's why I'm hoping you can help.
textByHash.2198ff039440e38a373d47166bcdc169d85c4cff: Bring me any barronite you find. In return, I can channel enough power from the Forge into the city or even open the Great Vault.
textByHash.ed0d9e1756316560918804cb3affc2dea1726be1: From what I can tell, my ancestors linked the Forge to the entire city.
textByHash.78d170c4019873fc1e35ee8d530b1ff8bd10ef2e: It truly is the heart of this place. From it, I can channel any barronite you have into empowering different aspects of the city.
textByHash.618ec207659184a99e7cea55d9aa1e6e6b1dec42: The Sacred Forge is a strange beast, but with its arcane power, you'll see an improvement to the various activities you can do in the city.
textByHash.8231b25f0723264ca3979d9a1dbc3f6d1c767b2c: Would you like to take a look?
textByHash.87d0a833737a4d4f2a48a897686ec8723463f096: Very well. Anything else you'd like to talk about?
textByHash.95142a0f94fe7b1fe6fbc814d06626540eb1473d: It's one of the many marvels of this city. We used it to store our greatest creations, along with our most interesting finds.
textByHash.b78c115ec686d5f796853c5031ff9004c912c145: Of course, I have no use for those treasures myself. I'd happily give them up to keep the Sacred Forge alive.
textByHash.366f6362c99be0abddd9034844c31d01c4721d67: Now be warned - I won't be able to channel much power into the Vault. I can't afford to waste too much barronite. You'll only have a limited amount of time in there.
textByHash.e8bc1741ada7fd0d4d4fd87400e2651b37455e91: Not only that, there's an automatic security system that activates if power to the Vault is lost. If that happens, you'll be removed. I imagine any treasures would be lost as well.
textByHash.a625faef59b674aa7a2f4a6eab02a321b40608d5: Hmm, for a minute, I suppose 750 barronite shards should do the trick.
textByHash.364577a31d1f4d8a3e395ed016e4e77f13aba809: If you do go in, I'd suggest heading to the back first. That's where the most valuable things are kept.
textByHash.b8c8141025b0214f57d5b4657abad9b9c84cce00: Is there anything else you wanted to talk about?
textByHash.87738b7385394508a88ff3a6a1031f931a7640ce: I'll be here if you change your mind. Take care .
textByHash.d8bb0d9826e13454a44cb3a874b71bb28437addf: A sword? Why do you need me to make you one?
textByHash.d4f308b0836be1a97d04280a2489faaf7567a314: I'm afraid there's not much I can do to help you . My lack of vision means I can't work an anvil like I used to.
textByHash.46868e71afdda38367e23cbd99ef35e3140a8dc3: They were probably talking about someone else anyway. I don't think many surface dwellers remember the dwarves who lived in Camdozaal.
textByHash.df6ee1c6344cc5a091adb4b9c9acbc2379e12e80: Ah, that looks like one of the relics from the civilization that lived here before Camdozaal was built.
textByHash.ed4bef8856ba36908c1c04cb30535214e2a48aab: I have no use for it. Maybe one of your kind would find it interesting.
textByHash.5008a3c397514e3f2ae6a3e77a33f7199724b7dc: Ah, a barronite deposit. You can use the anvils here to crack it open. If you're lucky, it might have something inside.
textByHash.90404b02c8a499328e95a386419997167ebc2edf: I could feed those shards into the Sacred Forge now, and keep their power for you to use later if you'd like?
textByHash.05c481aad4d68c1029d2c9d34dcac67474911618: Well there's a few things we could do with that.
textByHash.25ebdae1922100a3b69642d15be5f630aa8eb55e: Well just bring it back if you change your mind.
textByHash.5f5d43abb57b326f6537c34625911cd4108c70cf: That looks like part of a weapon. Hmm, I might be able to make something out of it. You'll need to have all the right pieces... and 1,500 barronite shards or if you'd prefer, I can smelt it down for Barronite shards?
textByHash.13ae7085508da2bdf1504e634c23b7d801d8da01: You need a Barronite and 1500 barronite shards.
textByHash.6ff0d53dd66ce25eeb21c599ebda37269cefdea4: Come back with all the parts, and I'll happily knock something up for you.
textByHash.e06bd2da2ccca14587f11cb0661052ca759b7e1b: You have all the parts I need. Give me a second, and I'll fix it up for you.
textByHash.7c35d0464d088fcc57328243f300c3ef63471ab8: There you go, one Barronite Mace. Should come in handy when killing the golems around here.
textByHash.af11dc4dab82ed2c704273be325728911474ee33: Alright then, I can smelt that down for 100 Barronite shards.
textByHash.45bc614ce4b07e2774784f65b2b619fbf96fa3fc: There you go. I can add those shards straight to the Forge as well if you'd like.
textByHash.2491d741c0cdcc631dcf606b86e5eed3b06a5df6: There you go, that's all done.
textByHash.bbd9d27c38ea9d1b11826dd40bd3dea2e41f419c: No problem, come back to me if you find anything else of interest.
textByHash.9e32f983d44880f1fb408fb9717736a3efa64eff: I reckon you could give one of those golems a nasty whack with that thing.
textByHash.e980df7f997f6739a712e5db2867980c17d9daa1: If you have no use for it, I could smelt it down for some Barronite shards?
textByHash.f80f9dd93bd9e73f750a87c653af6ff42135af27: Alright then, I can smelt that down for 500 Barronite shards.
textByHash.3afcc552a7e2c641025d6a4db79df3f99494ee2d: Ah, the Imcando Hammer. A very useful tool for Smithing and Construction. I could repair it for 1,250 barronite shards if you like or if you'd prefer, I can smelt it down for Barronite shards?
textByHash.57233aee9b5fff2af1fce0fa8698ec337fa5dda3: Come back with the necessary items, and I'll happily repair the Imcando Hammer for you.
textByHash.ec099b9f61263a04a7fac78778aed7352c95d38f: Give me a second, and I'll fix it up for you.
textByHash.07bb5abe1f9265ffc41b609b4bc13353933790e3: All done! Here is your Imcando Hammer.
textByHash.9621c12778aa5e714a76231167accd820d718628: Alright then, I can smelt that down for 350 Barronite shards.
textByHash.e7571b46d35a1558381bdb726f42df38b4c3049e: The Imcando Hammer, one of my favourite tools for Smithing and Construction.
textByHash.ddb4cfa52179c2466697d935806e673f77dd844f: Can't say I've ever had one.
textByHash.b3591fdea37f930ca61fc0b0207d860a3bd33dec: This is delicious! Where did you find such a excellent cuisine!
textByHash.e8b40e651d1ff42154ab6694ce628acbef56f609: You're an brilliant cook ! Just don't bring me too many of these, I'm trying to watch my weight.
